compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
RELATED ROBUSTNESS GAP (observed during the same incident): the review bot (src/rebar/review_bot/app.py) drains its in-memory asyncio.Queue with a SINGLE background worker. The per-event try/except only guards against exceptions — it does NOT guard against a HANG. When a review's clone/subprocess or LLM call blocks indefinitely (as happened when the disk was full mid-clone), that one worker is stuck forever on await, the queue silently backs up, and EVERY subsequent change (471, 472, plus other sessions' 461/462/474-478) gets no vote — no error, no log, just an inert bot accepting webhooks (202) and never processing. Recovery required a manual 'docker restart compose-review-bot-1' (which resets the worker + empties the in-memory queue) then re-/rerun of each change.
Suggested hardening (add to this bug's scope): (a) a per-review timeout (asyncio.wait_for around review_and_vote) so a hung review fails-closed to a coverage-gap vote instead of wedging the worker; (b) consider >1 worker or a watchdog; (c) the queue is in-memory, so a restart loses queued events — the backfill reconcile_loop should re-enqueue changes still lacking a current-patchset vote (verify it does).

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
The rebar review bot (compose-review-bot-1 on the rebar-gerrit EC2 host) clones each change into /tmp/rebar-gate-snapshots to review it. These snapshots are never garbage-collected, so the 30G root disk fills to 100%.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: review workspace is bounded / cleaned per review.
- Actual: /tmp/rebar-gate-snapshots grew to 14G; root fs hit 100%; clone_change_ref failed with 'No space left on device', which the voter surfaces as [LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap (review-error)] on EVERY change (observed on 452, 458, 461, 462). This fail-closed vetoes unrelated changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The review-bot's per-change clone/snapshot workspace is bounded or cleaned after each review (e.g. rm the snapshot on completion, or a periodic prune).
- [ ] A disk-usage guard/alarm so the host does not silently reach 100%.
- [ ] Document the manual recovery (docker builder prune + clear /tmp/rebar-gate-snapshots) in an infra runbook.

## Discovered
While landing epic breaded-ammonitic-elephant: change 458's LLM-Review persistently returned coverage-gap (review-error). Root cause via SSM was the full host disk, not the change.
compiled_state.title: review-bot host disk fills up (rebar-gate-snapshots leak) -> coverage-gap review-errors
